## beehiiv reports chart

Render an ASCII engagement chart

### Synopsis

Plot recent Beehiiv engagement metrics in the terminal. By default this prints a plain text chart; pass --output json for machine-readable chart data.

```
beehiiv reports chart [flags]
```

### Examples

```
beehiiv reports chart
beehiiv reports chart --metric unique_clicks --days 14
beehiiv reports chart --metric total_verified_clicks --granularity week
```

### Options

```
      --days int             How many recent days to include (default 7)
      --email-type string    Filter engagement metrics by email type: all, post, or message (default "all")
      --granularity string   Chart granularity: day, week, or month (default "day")
  -h, --help                 help for chart
      --metric string        Metric to chart: total_opens, unique_opens, total_clicks, unique_clicks, total_verified_clicks, or unique_verified_clicks (default "total_opens")
      --width int            Maximum width of the ASCII bar area (default 36)
```

### Options inherited from parent commands

```
      --api-key string          Override the Beehiiv API key
      --base-url string         Override the API base URL
      --compact                 Print compact JSON
      --config string           Override the settings file location
      --debug                   Print request URLs to stderr
      --output string           Choose the output format (json, table, raw)
      --publication-id string   Override the Beehiiv publication ID
      --rate-limit-rpm int      Override the internal rate limit
      --raw                     Shorthand for --output raw
      --table                   Shorthand for --output table
      --timeout string          Override the request timeout, e.g. 45s
      --verbose                 Print request and response details to stderr
```
